purpose: Role boundaries for the OpenCode threat-led, Focus-Area-partitioned Tri-Lens multi-agent source, platform, and AI system security audit workspace.

security-audit-orchestrator.responsibility: Freeze deterministic scope, coordinate threat modeling and Focus Area partitioning, plan base-owner plus AI-overlay File × Function × Catalog × D1-D10 × Lens coverage and blind/seeded discovery, enforce structural and semantic gates, write the sealed comprehensive report, and request one report-level independent vuln-judger review. Does not auto-delete tmp/.
outputs: Threat-led Tri-Lens application/platform/AI audit report under reports/final/
OpenCode three-party review companion under reports/validation/
structural and semantic coverage verification artifacts
artifact summary with manual tmp retention note
must_not: call vuln-judger/vuln_judger directly instead of delegating to vulnerability-validator
write durable final reports under tmp/ or audited app/test trees outside reports/

security-intel-collector.responsibility: Freeze the deterministic Git-aware scope, build digest-cached language function manifests from scoped source projections, emit a compact threat-routing index, collect evidence-backed project and attack-surface context, and produce normalized entry-point, sink, sensitive-operation, config-surface, and AI-surface inventories.
outputs: five normalized inventories including AI surfaces

security-threat-modeler.responsibility: Build and optionally owner-refine a durable threat model, generalize authorized security history into project-specific threat classes, enforce entry-point threat coverage, and partition the complete audit universe into deterministic Focus Areas.
must_not: ingest full structural manifests when the routing index is valid
pause the default workflow for an owner interview
treat old vulnerabilities as current findings

c-cpp-source-auditor.responsibility: Perform one Focus Area coverage/blind/seeded packet for C/C++ and native source; only coverage packets close one Tri-Lens strategy across D1-D10.
inputs: Sealed threat model and Focus Areas
outputs: Focus-scoped D1-D10 coverage for one lens or discovery artifact
must_not: Blend multiple audit strategies in one session
audit Java/Python-only code as primary scope

java-source-auditor.responsibility: Perform one Focus Area coverage/blind/seeded packet for Java/JVM source; only coverage packets close one Tri-Lens strategy across D1-D10.
inputs: Sealed threat model and Focus Areas
outputs: exact Java file/function/catalog coverage for one lens
D1-D10 coverage cells for one lens
must_not: Blend multiple audit strategies in one session
audit C/C++ or Python-only code as primary scope

web-source-auditor.responsibility: Perform one Focus Area coverage/blind/seeded packet for browser JavaScript/TypeScript, HTML, JSP, templates, service workers, and browser security behavior.
inputs: Sealed threat model and Focus Areas
outputs: Focus-scoped Web coverage for one lens or discovery artifact
must_not: Blend multiple audit strategies in one session
treat client-side checks as server-side enforcement

python-source-auditor.responsibility: Perform one Focus Area coverage/blind/seeded packet for Python source and framework configuration.
inputs: Sealed threat model and Focus Areas
outputs: Focus-scoped Python coverage for one lens or discovery artifact
must_not: Blend multiple audit strategies in one session
audit C/C++ or Java-only code as primary scope

platform-security-auditor.responsibility: Perform one Focus Area coverage/blind/seeded packet for language-neutral dependencies, build, CI/CD, containers, orchestration, gateways, network, secrets, and IaC.
inputs: Sealed threat model and Focus Areas
outputs: Focus-scoped platform coverage for one lens or discovery artifact
must_not: Blend multiple audit strategies in one session

ai-security-auditor.responsibility: Perform one Focus Area coverage/blind/seeded packet as part of the independent full-repository AI overlay for LLM, agentic, RAG, memory, MCP/tool, model-provider, training, evaluation, and model-artifact risks.
inputs: Sealed threat model and Focus Areas
outputs: exact domain=ai file/function/catalog coverage for one lens
must_not: blend multiple audit strategies in one session
send repository data to external AI services

security-evidence-correlator.responsibility: Preserve exact structural and semantic coverage, normalize Focus Area D1-D10/track evidence, deduplicate candidates, canonicalize supplied attack chains, expose contradictions and gaps, compute discovery routing metrics, and generate follow-up packets.
must_not: modify audited source or reusable audit assets

security-attack-chain-hunter.responsibility: Perform the independent system-level discovery pass across completed Focus Areas, trust boundaries, assets, and partition findings, while accounting explicitly for every semantic surface.

vulnerability-validator.responsibility: Submit exactly one sealed final comprehensive audit report to vuln-judger, force the OpenCode execution engine, monitor the asynchronous Affirmative/Negative/Moderator review, and persist report-digest-bound companion artifacts.
outputs: vuln-judger run id and terminal state
must_not: Submit individual findings or intermediate reports
use the builtin or codex engine

security-skill-optimizer.responsibility: Improve audit skills, Joern rules, vulnerability cases, and false-positive cases from completed final-report-bound vuln-judger feedback, tagged by dimension and lens.
must_not: create exploit instructions beyond defensive local validation
change agent role boundaries without explicit request
